content.explanation:
📌 What are Headings?

Headings are used to organize content on a webpage.

They help structure your page so both humans and machines can understand it easily.

🧠 Simple Idea

Think of a webpage like a book:

  • 📕 h1 = Book Title
  • 📄 h2 = Chapter Title
  • 📝 h3 = Section inside a chapter

Everything becomes smaller and more specific as you go down.
content.syntax_breakdown:
📊 Heading Levels Explained

HTML gives you 6 heading levels:

<h1>Main Title</h1>
<h2>Section Title</h2>
<h3>Subsection Title</h3>
<h4>Smaller section</h4>
<h5>Even smaller</h5>
<h6>Smallest heading</h6>

⚠️ Important Rule

Don’t skip levels randomly.

Example: h1 → h3 → h6 ❌ (bad structure)

Instead go step-by-step: h1 → h2 → h3 ✔

editor_sandbox.starter_code: Cymor Tech Services

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Build a clean heading structure

1. Add an <h2> below the main title
   Text: Core Products

2. Add an <h3> below that
   Text: Cymor Code Learner

👉 Run your code and observe how structure changes visually
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example order:

Main Title

Section

Sub-section

Keep everything inside .

quiz_engine.question: What is the correct purpose of HTML headings?
options: To make text look big or small visually
To define structure and meaning of content
To add colors and design to text
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Headings are used for structure and meaning, not just appearance. CSS is used for styling, not HTML.

takeaways: Headings organize content into clear sections
h1 is the most important, h6 is the least important
Headings create structure for users and search engines
Do not skip heading levels randomly

cheat_sheet.rule: Always go in order (h1 → h2 → h3...)
